title: Subqueries, CTEs, Set Operations, and Relational Reasoning · Mastery Check
summary: Operations needs reusable query stages for backlog comparisons, missing relationships, and cohorts across time windows. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a composable backlog-analysis query pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose scalar, row, and table subqueries whose cardinality matches their surrounding expression.
Distinguish correlated subqueries and use EXISTS or NOT EXISTS for semijoin and antijoin questions.
Use CTEs to name query stages while distinguishing readability from materialization guarantees.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;
